claim: The File.* HTTP surface crosses a crate boundary with byte-identical OpenAPI on BOTH surfaces.
evidence: SPIKE regen ui+desktop vs .extraction/baseline: types.ui.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL, types.desktop.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL, openapi.ui.json CANONICALLY-EQUAL, openapi.desktop.json CANONICALLY-EQUAL. Held by: DTOs schemars-keyed by short ident (already ziee-file-owned or moved verbatim); with_permission emits files::* CONST strings; RequirePermissions<R,_> OperationIo is input-only + R-independent; operationIds are literals in the moved *_docs; emit_ts sorts endpoints/schemas + golden compares jq -S canonical, so the router split is order-independent.

claim: Splitting the router across two crates (SDK bundle + ziee residue) preserves every path+method, incl. the same-path GET/POST and GET/DELETE merges.
evidence: axum merges method routers on a shared path. Regen confirms /api/files/{file_id}/versions = {get(list_versions, SDK), post(append_version, ziee)} and /api/files/{file_id} = {get, delete}. All 17 moved + 6 retained routes present; golden byte-identical.

claim: The unauthenticated download-token path keeps its full revocation re-check (is_active + current files::download union) after the move.
evidence: download_with_token STAYED ziee-side verbatim (the only change: re-importing the moved content_disposition + FILE_CONTENT_CACHE_CONTROL from the SDK). Its Repos.user.get_by_id + is_active + check_permission_union('files::download') + iss/aud=DOWNLOAD_TOKEN_AUDIENCE validation are unchanged — a disabled account / revoked perm still fails (audit 05-file F-06/F-03 preserved). The token SIGNER (generate_download_token, moved) and VERIFIER (download_with_token, ziee) share issuer+secret via config.jwt → FileContext.download_token vs get_jwt_config, both seeded from the same config.jwt.

claim: Every moved handler stays owner-scoped (cross-user id → 404), no perm relaxation.
evidence: All moved reads/writes call get_by_id_and_user / get_version(...,user_id) / list_by_user(user_id) unchanged; auth.user.id is the acting id from RequirePermissions<R,_>. Permission gates unchanged (FilesRead/Preview/Download/Delete/Upload/GenerateToken), enforced by the same framework extractor + ZieeIdentityResolver. content_disposition CRLF-injection guard moved verbatim.

claim: The SDK file surface names zero ziee-domain symbol.
evidence: grep of sdk/crates/ziee-file/src/http: no crate::core / Repos / Config / sync::publish / file_rag / ProcessingManager / pandoc / workflow. Only injected inputs: RequirePermissions<R,_>, Extension<FileContext>, ziee_framework::sync::SyncOrigin, ziee_auth::{User,Group}. sync/RAG reached ONLY through the FileEvents seam.

claim: The store surface's app dependencies invert through one FileContext (+ the pre-existing FileEvents seam).
evidence: FileContext{files: Arc<FileRepository>, events: Arc<dyn FileEvents>, download_token: DownloadTokenSigner}. build_file_context installs ZieeFileEvents (forwarding to sync::publish_* + spawn_reindex) + issuer/secret from config.jwt. Layered as Extension at BOTH server-setup sites (main.rs bin block + lib.rs setup_server for desktop/openapi-gen).

claim: restore_version's sync + RAG side-effects are byte-behaviour-identical after routing through the seam.
evidence: Original: publish_file_changed_with_origin(user,file,origin.0) + spawn_reindex(user,file). Moved: ctx.events.on_file_changed(user,file,origin.0) + ctx.events.on_committed(user,file,false). ZieeFileEvents.on_file_changed → publish_file_changed_with_origin; on_committed(is_new=false) → spawn_reindex. Same funcs, same args, same order.

claim: External consumers of the moved symbols compile unchanged.
evidence: Only cross-module consumer of a moved symbol is chat::core::export → crate::modules::file::handlers::download::content_disposition, preserved by the ziee download.rs `pub use ziee_file::http::content_disposition`. upload_file_inner (project_extension) is on the still-ziee upload path. cargo check -p ziee = 0, -p ziee-desktop = 0.

claim: All three build gates pass with no new warnings.
evidence: cargo check -p ziee = 0, -p ziee-desktop = 0, (cd sdk && cargo check --workspace) = 0. The bin 'build_file_context is never used' warning was cleared by adding the main.rs layer. Remaining 3 warnings (KB_MAX_DOCUMENTS_DEFAULT, is_active, list_enabled_for_health_check) are pre-existing + unrelated (present at baseline).

claim: ziee-file compiles STANDALONE with the routes feature (sdk workspace + -p ziee-file).
evidence: Added aide `axum-query` feature (supplies OperationInput for axum::extract::Query — the workspace-level aide the server links already had it, so the full-build feature union is unchanged). cargo check -p ziee-file = 0; sdk workspace = 0.

This chunk moves only HTTP handlers/routes; zero migration files touched (ziee-file already carries its base files/file_versions schema from chunk ziee-file). The file-only build DB story is unchanged (routes add no query! macros beyond the engine's).

claim: Only store-generic handlers moved; processing/identity-coupled stayed ziee-side, each justified.
evidence: STAYED: upload_file (ProcessingManager+quota+RAG), export_file (pandoc), append_version (commit_new_version→ProcessingManager), download_with_token (by-id identity re-check), deliverables (chat domain). MOVED: the 17 read/serve/delete + version-read + restore + download + token-gen handlers. See CUT.md 'Stays ziee-side' + TRANSFORMS Decisions 1-2.
